framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Public signal-integrity / EMI mastery through teaching
paths.what_it_is: Building deep, rigorous expertise in signal integrity, EMI, and PCB noise control, then codifying it into publicly accessible courses, textbooks, and conference talks that other engineers rely on.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves an engineer's understanding is precise enough to teach and defend in public, not just apply privately — the rarest and most trusted form of technical credibility in the field.

paths.what_it_is: Systematically opening up hardware — chips, instruments, vintage and modern devices — and publishing detailed, rigorous write-ups of how the internals actually work.
paths.why_it_signals: It demonstrates the ability to read and explain real circuits from the ground up, a form of hands-on technical fluency that is hard to fake and highly visible to peers.

paths.path: Open-hardware project leadership with public PCB documentation
paths.what_it_is: Leading an open-hardware project or design practice and publishing the schematics, PCB layouts, and design-decision rationale for others to study and build on.
paths.why_it_signals: It shows an engineer can carry a design from concept to a manufacturable, well-documented product — and is confident enough in the work to expose it to public scrutiny.
